## Opinion: Meta stock appears attractively valued despite heavy AI spending

The recurring debate over META's AI spend justification is the central valuation tension for the name; if the market increasingly accepts the 'cheap despite capex' framing, it could support multiple expansion — but the thesis ultimately requires AI monetization to show up in revenue, not just in cost efficiency arguments.

- An opinion piece argues META's valuation remains attractive even accounting for elevated AI investment.
- No new financial data, guidance changes, or catalysts are cited — this is purely a valuation framing piece.
- The 'cheap despite heavy spending' argument reinforces the ongoing hyperscaler capex-vs-return debate.
- Medium confidence; directionally positive on META but absent hard new evidence.
